properties.remark: *** 2 INJ *** A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3 MILES EAST SOUTHEAST OF ALMA. TWO MOBILE HOMES WERE SEVERELY DAMAGED...LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND OTHER STRUCTURES HAD SUFFERED DAMAGE. THE PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 6.2 MILES WITH A WIDTH OF 30 TO 50 YARDS.
*** 2 INJ *** POSSIBLE TORNADO. HIGHWAY 32 EAST ABOUT 3 MILES EAST OF ALMA IN THE ROCKINGHAM COMMUNITY. MOBILE HOME DESTROYED...OTHER STRUCTURES DAMAGED. SEVERAL TREES DOWN.

NWS JACKSONVILLE CONDUCTED A STORM SURVEY IN COFFEE COUNTY GEORGIA ON THE OUTSKIRTS OF DOUGLAS. THE TORNADO WAS DETERMINED TO BE AN EF0 WITH WINDS OF 75 TO 85 MPH. THE LENGTH OF THE TORNADO WAS APPROXIMATELY 4 MILES IN LENGTH AND 60 YARDS AT ITS PEAK WIDTH. THERE WAS SOME DAMAGE TO HOUSE SIDING, SHALLOW ROOTED PINES WERE PUSHED OVER, LARGE BRANCHES WERE BROKEN OFF TREES, MINOR ROOF DAMAGE, AND LOSS OF WOOD AND METAL PANELS ON SMALL BARNS AND FARM OUTBUILDINGS.

THE WARNING COORDINATION METEOROLOGIST CONDUCTED A FLOOD SURVEY ALONG MCCOYS CREEK IN DOWNTOWN JACKSONVILLE AND DETERMINED THAT FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED DURING THE EVENING AND OVERNIGHT HOURS ON SUNDAY AS RUNOFF FROM HEAVY RAINFALL WAS BACKED UP BY THE EVENING HIGH TIDE.

EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED WIND DAMAGE AT THE CENTRAL HAMILTON ELEMENTARY SCHOOL. MULTIPLE POWER LINES DOWN WITH ONE POWER POLE LANDING ON A BUILDING. ROOF BLOWN OFF ONE OF THE SCHOOL BUILDINGS. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED AND THE SCHOOL WAS EVACUATED AFTER THE EVENT.
